study_goal: Drive the AA leader PUT benchmark to higher E2E throughput by progressively relaxing benchmark-side limits and the server max.poll.records cap; identify the new bottleneck at higher drive rate.
completion_status: COMPLETE — all 5 experiments executed, criterion 6 (OFF/ON sanity) and criterion 7 (integration tests) both PASS.

criteria_status.criterion_1_baseline_reconfirmed: PASS — captured at dc538f916, see phase3_baseline_median
criteria_status.criterion_2_min_5_experiments: PASS — 5 experiments executed (baseline, Exp.1 A, Exp.2 C, Exp.3 A+C, Exp.4 A+C+B-2, Exp.5 A+C+B-4)
criteria_status.criterion_4_new_bottleneck_named: PASS — bottleneck shifts to dcr_merge in Exp.4 and remains so in Exp.5
criteria_status.criterion_6_off_on_sanity: PASS — Exp.4 OFF median 113,714 ops/s vs ON median 112,044 ops/s (delta +1.49%, within +/-5%)
criteria_status.criterion_7_integration_tests: PASS — 16 tests total (11 ActiveActiveReplicationForHybridTest + 5 TestActiveActiveIngestion), 0 failures, 0 errors
criteria_status.criterion_8_artifacts: PASS — this JSON + the report MD are written; benchmark code change committed

experiments.config: NUM_RECORDS_PER_INVOCATION=1000, max.poll.records=100, single producer per region, single sender thread (Phase 2 HEAD dc538f916, no benchmark mods)

experiments.decision: REJECT — bigger invocations didn't move E2E median (essentially flat at +1%); leader_idle climbed (66.93%) because each invocation's send burst is 10x longer in absolute time, widening the inter-burst gap
MARGINAL — +3% throughput; leader_record_wall_avg dropped (42us vs 60us) because consumer batches are now bigger per poll, but empty-poll % remained ~91% (producer side still throttled)

REJECT — combined A+C REGRESSED throughput by 7.9%; leader_record_wall_avg climbed to 70us (likely cache-line / working-set effects from larger batches), and the higher per-record CPU offset the throughput gains

experiments.config: NUM_RECORDS_PER_INVOCATION=10000, max.poll.records=1000, 2 producers per region, 2 sender threads per region (4 total producers)
experiments.decision: KEEP / WINNER — +19.6% E2E throughput. The new bottleneck is dcr_merge (40.7% of leader wall). RocksDB RMD reads collapsed to 0.16% because higher write density makes the transient record cache effective (99.99% inferred hit rate vs <1% in Phase 1/2). leader_record_wall_avg drops dramatically (60us -> 7us) due to skipping most RocksDB reads.

experiments.config: NUM_RECORDS_PER_INVOCATION=10000, max.poll.records=1000, 4 producers per region, 4 sender threads per region (8 total producers)
experiments.decision: REJECT (no further gain) — doubling producers from 4 to 8 yields essentially flat E2E (-0.5% vs Exp.4, within run-to-run noise). Producer-side aggregate rate doubled (~217k -> ~496k/s) and buffer_full events climbed (16 -> 85 per window), but the leader pool can only sink ~112k/s regardless. Confirms the new bottleneck is server-side dcr_merge, NOT producer throughput. leader_idle cumulative across pool grew (675% -> 766%), showing more pool slack at higher producer count without translating to higher E2E.

headline_finding.winning_config: Exp.4 A+C+B-2 (NUM_RECORDS_PER_INVOCATION=10000, max.poll.records=1000, 2 producers per region with 2 sender threads per region)
headline_finding.secondary_finding: Multi-producer drive density makes the existing transient record cache effective: at higher write rate, RocksDB RMD reads drop from ~42% of leader wall to 0.16%. The ~99.99% transient-cache hit rate is a NEW regime not seen at lower drive rates (Phase 1 measured 0.4-5.5%).
headline_finding.tertiary_finding_exp5: Doubling producer count from 4 to 8 (Exp.5) does NOT yield further E2E gain (-0.5% vs Exp.4), confirming the bottleneck has moved decisively server-side to dcr_merge. Producer-side aggregate doubles to ~496k/s but is absorbed by the leader pool's slack (cumulative leader_idle climbs from 675% to 766%); E2E plateau at ~112k ops/s on this hardware.
